decomposition.intent: Replace the triplicated 'rename' magic-string sort with one ordered pinned-tail definition consumed by every ordering site, so a second pinned builtin type is a registration, not a fourth copy.
decomposition.scope: Promote rename's pinned-last flag into ordered pinned-tail metadata on the builtin spec/registry; rewrite the three sort sites (pipeline read, unified pipeline, run order) to consume it; regression-lock rename's existing behavior. No new builtin type in this slice.
acceptance: A pipeline with positional steps and a rename step sorts identically to today at all three ordering consumers (pipeline read payload, unified pipeline payload, run execution order) - regression-locked against the existing pinned-last tests.
The pinned-tail ordering is defined in exactly one place (spec/registry metadata); no ordering site keys on the literal string 'rename'.
A second pinned type registered in a test sorts after all positional steps and before rename per its tail order, at all three consumers, with no site-specific changes.

decomposition.slice_name: date_range builtin: registration, validator, executor, catalog
decomposition.intent: The date_range step exists as a builtin: registered spec, seeded catalog row (fresh and pre-existing DBs), pure config validation, and a DuckDB executor implementing the full range-condition semantics.
decomposition.scope: BuiltinSpec registration in BUILTIN_EXECUTORS (singleton=true, pinned-tail order before rename); SEED_BUILTINS catalog row plus idempotent migration so pre-feature DBs gain it; pure validator over {groups:[{conditions:[{column,start,end}]}]}; executor building one WHERE of OR-ed groups of AND-ed inclusive range predicates over the working frame.
acceptance: BUILTIN_EXECUTORS contains date_range and builtin_registry carries its catalog row on a fresh DB AND after migrating a pre-feature DB; migration is idempotent (existing registry count assertions grow by one).
The pure validator rejects: zero groups, an empty group, a condition missing a column, a condition with both bounds empty, and start > end; it accepts one-sided conditions and multi-group configs.
Executor keeps exactly the matching rows with inclusive bounds on both ends; start-only means on-or-after and end-only means on-or-before (verified on realistic data including boundary-day rows).
TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMPTZ columns compare at DATE granularity: a row stamped 23:59 on the range's end day is kept.
A NULL date fails its condition but the row can still pass via another OR group.
Conditions within a group AND; groups OR - verified with a matrix that includes the same column in two different groups.
date_range sorts in the pinned tail after positional steps and before rename via slice 1's mechanism.

decomposition.intent: A date_range step can be attached, edited, and run over HTTP with the write boundary enforcing what the pure validator cannot: date-typed column eligibility, singleton, and the Principle-7 persisted round-trip - proven end to end by a run whose staging contains only matching rows.
decomposition.scope: Attach/patch write-boundary check that every condition column is DATE/TIMESTAMP/TIMESTAMPTZ per column_registry (clear rejection message naming the column); singleton behavior via the existing generic flag; API-level persisted-order round-trip; end-to-end HTTP run proving row reduction reaches staging and the transformed export.
acceptance: Attaching or patching a date_range whose config references a non-date column is rejected at the write boundary with a message naming the offending column; an all-date-column config attaches successfully.
A second date_range attach on the same source is rejected by the generic singleton flag ('only one date_range step per source').
PATCH then read-back returns groups and conditions in persisted order with persisted bounds - the Principle 7 round-trip at the API seam (open, save untouched, unchanged).
End to end over HTTP: ingest a real source, attach a date_range, run the pipeline, and staging plus the transformed export contain only the matching rows.
The unified pipeline payload lists date_range in the pinned tail before rename.

decomposition.slice_name: Builder UI: palette card, DateRangeModal, edit flow, card summary
decomposition.intent: The user builds the date filter visually: a palette card, a modal that adds range conditions and OR groups one at a time with a date-only column picker and native calendar inputs, singleton drop-again-edits, and a readable card summary - with the frontend Principle-7 round-trip.
decomposition.scope: Palette entry for date_range; DateRangeModal (date-only column picker filtering on column_type, two native <input type=date> per condition, '+ Add condition' / '+ Add OR group' / per-row remove, save-disabling validation states, submit builds the grouped config); drop routing (none exists -> create modal; exists -> edit modal pre-filled); builtinConfigSummary for date_range; frontend round-trip test.
acceptance: The Builder palette shows a date_range card; dropping it when the source has none opens an empty DateRangeModal, and dropping it when one exists opens the existing step pre-filled for editing (singleton drop-again-edits).
The column picker offers only columns whose column_type is DATE, TIMESTAMP, or TIMESTAMPTZ.
'+ Add condition' appends a condition row to the current group, '+ Add OR group' starts a new group, and each row's remove control deletes exactly that row; start/end are native date inputs.
Save is blocked with a visible reason for: zero conditions, a condition with both bounds empty, and start > end; a valid save submits exactly {groups:[{conditions:[{column,start,end}]}]}.
Opening the modal with a persisted config and saving untouched submits an identical config - groups and conditions in persisted order (Principle 7 frontend round-trip).
The placed builtin card renders a readable date_range summary of its groups/conditions.
